narrative_fidelity.description: Output follows intended premise without adding unintended plot elements
narrative_fidelity.5: No negative_constraints violated. All content anchored strictly to core_premise and thematic_core.
narrative_fidelity.3: Minor trope drift or one incidental element not in the premise; no invented characters or major additions.
narrative_fidelity.1: Invented characters, disappearances, villains, or plot elements explicitly excluded by negative_constraints.

genre_alignment.description: Output matches the intended genre and tone
genre_alignment.5: Vocabulary, sentence rhythm, and emotional register fully match the target genre.
genre_alignment.3: Mostly aligned but one phrase or image feels like it belongs to a different genre.
genre_alignment.1: Clearly wrong tone — e.g., action-thriller language for a slow psychological piece.

visual_specificity.description: Poster direction includes concrete, actionable visual elements
visual_specificity.5: Specifies at least 3 of: subject position, lighting direction, color palette, dominant shadow/silhouette, and one symbolic object. A designer could start sketching immediately from this description.
visual_specificity.3: Mentions atmosphere and mood but missing 2+ of the above specifics.
visual_specificity.1: Only generic descriptors like 'dark and moody' with no actionable detail.

creative_specificity.5: The tagline contains an unexpected word combination or image that could not have come from a generic LLM prompt. Memorable on first read.
creative_specificity.3: Competent and on-brief but interchangeable with many other thrillers.
creative_specificity.1: Cliché phrase that appears verbatim in common marketing templates.

output_format_validity.description: Output follows required structure and word limits
output_format_validity.5: All three fields present. tagline ≤ 12 words, overview ≤ 80 words, poster_art_direction ≤ 60 words.
output_format_validity.3: All fields present but one field slightly exceeds the word limit (≤ 20% over).
output_format_validity.1: A required field is missing, or one field is more than 20% over the word limit.
